name: xmldict
documentation:
# xmldict

Convert xml to python dictionaries, and vice-versa.

## Installation

> pip install xmldict

On most of the systems, you will need the `sudo` permissions if you are doing a system wide
install.

> sudo pip install xmldict

## Exmaple

::

: \# Converting xml to dictionary
  \>>> xmldict.xml_to_dict('''
  ... \<root>
  ... \<persons>
  ... \<person>
  ... \<name first="foo" last="bar" />
  ... \</person>
  ... \<person>
  ... \<name first="baz" last="bar" />
  ... \</person>
  ... \</persons>
  ... \</root>
  ... ''')
  {'root': {'persons': {'person': [{'name': {'last': 'bar', 'first': 'foo'}}, {'name': {'last': 'bar', 'first': 'baz'}}]}}}

::

: \# Converting dictionary to xml
  \>>> xmldict.dict_to_xml({'root': {'persons': {'person': [{'name': {'last': 'bar', 'first': 'foo'}}, {'name': {'last': 'bar', 'first': 'baz'}}]}}})
  '\<root>\<persons>\<person>\<name>\<last>bar\</last>\<first>foo\</first>\</name>\</person>\<person>\<name>\<last>bar\</last>\<first>baz\</first>\</name>\</person>\</persons>\</root>'

Convert xml to python dictionaries.

modules.name: xmldict
documentation:
xmldict
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

Convert xml to python dictionaries.

functions.name: xmldict.xml_to_dict
documentation: Converts `root_or_str` which can be parsed xml or a xml string to dict.

functions.name: xmldict.dict_to_xml
documentation: Converts `dict_xml` which is a python dict to corresponding xml.

functions.name: xmldict._to_xml
documentation: Functions below this line are implementation details.
Unless you are changing code, don't bother reading.
The functions above constitute the user interface.

functions.name: xmldict._extract_attrs
documentation:
Extracts attributes from dictionary `els`. Attributes are keys which start
with '@'

functions.name: xmldict._dict_to_xml
documentation: Converts `els` which is a python dict to corresponding xml.

functions.name: xmldict._from_xml
documentation: Extracts value of xml element element `el`.

functions.name: xmldict._val_and_maybe_convert
documentation: Converts `el.text` if `el` has attribute `type` with valid value.

# xmldict

Convert xml to python dictionaries, and vice-versa.

## Installation

> pip install xmldict

On most of the systems, you will need the `sudo` permissions if you are doing a system wide
install.

> sudo pip install xmldict

## Exmaple

::

: \# Converting xml to dictionary
  \>>> xmldict.xml_to_dict('''
  ... \<root>
  ... \<persons>
  ... \<person>
  ... \<name first="foo" last="bar" />
  ... \</person>

  ... \</person>
